answer.text: Past closing prices of the underlying asset

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Historical volatility uses PAST price data (closing prices) to calculate standard deviation of returns. Implied volatility is derived from current option PRICES. Forecasted volatility uses statistical models. **The Trap:** IV comes from option prices (market-based). HV comes from historical price data (statistical). **Cross-Exam Context:** Volatility types are tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When IV > HV, options are considered 'expensive'. When IV < HV, options are considered 'cheap'. This helps in deciding whether to buy or sell options.
